subject: [Pre-read] CB FP&A monthly Initiatives and Update_Jun'25

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find tmr's FP&A monthly meeting, we will be covering following topics


*CB Performance update*

   - May’25 Actuals Performance (as of Jun 6th) - with mgmt reporting view
   and SLS true cost savings view
   - Jun’25 Projection - with KRJP breakdown

*Key Initiatives Updates*

   - All BU: CB P&L Projection Accuracy Improvement Initiatives
   - All BU: CB Seller Universe current and Year End performance
   - Marketplace/Choice: June Finalized Commission Change Update
      - Anbo warehouse commission and business follow-up
      - CN new merchant commission waiving proposal
   - Marketplace: May’25 Commission Waive Program Update
   - SLS: MY Self Collection Channel Push study

*Besides, next Thursday will be Reg/Local FP&A workshop, where CB is
invited for a sharing, and kindly seek your review on if any adjustments
are needed! Key purpose as follows,*

   - *Local Ownership Initiative* Review and* re-emphasize importance *of
   timely, careful, and accurate submission requirements
   - Collect constructive feedbacks on how to collaborate better future

Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Slide 9: For SLS logic errors, I remember we saw that in  MY, TH and BR in
May?

Slide 23: So the key issue here is ESF is too low. True cost difference is
$0.10, but ESF difference is $0.67.

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - For 1P + Consignment, what is the latest alignment?

The losses here
   are quite huge?

Slide 5

   - Same question for 1P + consignment
   - Why is KR missing ADO targets?
- For SIP, our ADO is higher than May but EBITDA is lower?

Slide 9

   - Which markets are ok/improving, which markets are not?
- Can remind me whether SLS ownership is held in local or in CN?

Slide 12

   - Is it easy to fake being a CNLS seller right now?

Slide 19

   - Think it is ok for new to site if we want more sellers in more sites?
- Why do we need to include SIP for this?

I am not quite sure what this is about
   - And how does this compare with SG which is also big in BSC?

- We will be reducing BSC to 0 for MY moving forward, what will the
   impact be here?

all the markets. With that, we can avoid mistakes happened in one market
appear in other markets.

Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find reply to your questions below.

Slide 4 Kuan Wai Mei <waimei.kuan@shopee.com>

   -

   [Ian] For 1P + Consignment, what is the latest alignment? The losses
   here are quite huge?


   -

   1) commission to be -2% vs CNCB SLS post commission increase rate
   -

   2) SLS not to adjust in system, for CB internal view to use last
   alignment ~5% discount vs current ~29% discount
   -

   3) SCS target reported to external will be moderate version (Jun’25
   target - moderate (and external view) is 255k RR 108%, while optimistic
   (and internal view) is 278k RR 98%.

Big losses mainly in MP PC2, seller P&L breakeven and MoM UE stabilized

   -

   For MP PC2, why is it very negative?
   -

      Mainly due to high ADGMV% penetration in BR where CB is also negative
      -

   For MP PC2 MoM changes, +89k MoM vs May’25, improvement is driven by:
   -

      Lower MP Revenue from reduced platform fees (net off base commission
      reduction from Anbo rate and increased fees in SG/MY/VN/PH),
this reduction
      have been reflected as improvement in seller P&L (+315k USD MoM
vs May’25 )
      -

      Offset against lower PRM spending, CIR% -22%, +0.5% MoM vs May’25

Table - Jun’25 MP PC2 and MP PC2% by markets


Slide 5 Kuan Wai Mei <waimei.kuan@shopee.com> Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>

   -

   [Ian] Same question for 1P + consignment >> addressed above
   -

   [Ian] Why is KR missing ADO targets? Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com> Yuna
   Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>

performance, KR ADO runrate expect to land at 103% runrate, will monitor
closely


   -

   [Ian] For SIP, our ADO is higher than May but EBITDA is lower? How
come? Steven
   Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>

push(MoM +8%), incremental ROI is -22% which is below regional guideline of
50%



Slide 9 Edric Tan Eng Tong <edric.tanet@shopee.com>
